text: {"suggestedLabel":"Skeptical View on Egg Cardiovascular Risk","suggestedDescription":"A cautious perspective that treats risk‑related claims as uncertain and emphasizes confounding evidence.","stances":[{"claimIndex":0,"stance":"accept","rationale":"Eggs are indeed a cheap source of protein and nutrients, so we acknowledge that fact."},{"claimIndex":1,"stance":"accept","rationale":"It is well‑established that chicken eggs contain a relatively high amount of cholesterol."},{"claimIndex":2,"stance":"uncertain","rationale":"The claim that egg cholesterol behaves differently from other dietary cholesterol lacks decisive proof and is confounded by many variables."},{"claimIndex":3,"stance":"uncertain","rationale":"We cannot rule out that accompanying foods, not the eggs themselves, drive any observed heart‑disease signals."},{"claimIndex":4,"stance":"uncertain","rationale":"Cooking methods introduce many other risk factors, making it unclear whether eggs per se are responsible."},{"claimIndex":5,"stance":"uncertain","rationale":"Evidence that most healthy people can safely eat seven eggs weekly is mixed and heavily confounded, so we remain non‑committal."},{"claimIndex":6,"stance":"reject","rationale":"The assertion that seven eggs a week raise heart‑disease risk for diabetics is not supported by robust, unconfounded data."},{"claimIndex":7,"stance":"uncertain","rationale":"While some studies found no link for diabetics, the overall picture is inconsistent, so we stay uncertain."},{"claimIndex":8,"stance":"uncertain","rationale":"The idea that egg consumption may cause diabetes is speculative and entangled with lifestyle factors."},{"claimIndex":9,"stance":"reject","rationale":"Recommendations to cap dietary cholesterol at 300 mg are based on outdated assumptions and lack strong contemporary support."},{"claimIndex":10,"stance":"accept","rationale":"A large egg does contain roughly 186 mg of cholesterol, all located in the yolk."},{"claimIndex":11,"stance":"uncertain","rationale":"The notion that an egg a day is safe when other cholesterol sources are low remains unproven amid many confounders."},{"claimIndex":12,"stance":"accept","rationale":"Egg whites provide protein without any cholesterol, a clear factual statement."}]}
